steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to provide a specific example of irrational juxtaposition in surrealist collage. "Specific" means the answer must name the actual elements being juxtaposed — not just label a piece as an example or describe the concept in general terms.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Images of a young couple, a boat made of leaves, and a vast celestial space are juxtaposed irrationally in the surrealist collage piece *Vagabonds* by artist Felipe Posada.": Names all three specific elements being juxtaposed and identifies them as irrational juxtaposition in a named work. This is the concrete example the question demands. ✓
  • "The irrational juxtaposition of disparate elements in Felipe Posada's art invokes "a space where intuition, memories, visions, and dreams are free to take *Vagabonds*."": Misquotes the artist's words and omits the actual elements being juxtaposed. It references irrational juxtaposition abstractly, not with specific details. ✗
  • "Surrealist collage, such as Felipe Posada's *Vagabonds*, manifests the unconscious mind and irrationally juxtaposes disparate elements.": Identifies the piece and mentions irrational juxtaposition as a concept, but never names the specific elements juxtaposed in Vagabonds. ✗
  • "*Vagabonds* by artist Felipe Posada is an example of surrealist collage, a form in which irrational juxtaposition is a central aspect.": Only identifies the piece and defines the art form; gives no specific juxtaposed elements at all. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
